title: Method and apparatus for controlling a user's interaction with a non-medical application
abstract: A method and an apparatus for controlling a user's interaction with a non-medical application, such as a computer game, have been disclosed. The method comprises steps of providing a skin conductance signal measured at an area of the user's skin through a time interval, counting the number of fluctuation peaks in the skin conductance signal through said time interval, and using the number of fluctuation peaks in the skin conductance signal as a control parameter for controlling the user's interaction with the non-medical application.
claims: Claims 1. Method for controlling a user' s interaction with a non-medical application, the method comprising providing a skin conductance signal measured at an area of the user' s skin through a time interval, counting the number of fluctuation peaks in the skin conductance signal through said time interval, using the number of fluctuation peaks in the skin conductance signal as a control parameter for controlling the user's interaction with the non-medical application. 2. Method according to claim 1, wherein the non-medical application is a computer game. 3. Method according to claim 2, wherein the control parameter controls one, two or three dimensional movement of a moveable object in the game. 4. Method according to claim 3, wherein the movement of the moveable object is controlled forwardly or backwardly in dependency if the control parameter. 5. Method according to claim 3, wherein a speed of the moveable object is controlled in dependency of the control parameter. 6. Method according to claim 2, wherein an educational learning objective of the computer game is changed in dependency of the control parameter. 7. Method according to claim 2, wherein the computer game is a multi-player game. 8. Method according to claim 1, wherein the step of providing a skin conductance signal includes measuring a skin conductance on a palmar side of a hand of the user. 9. Method according to claim 1, wherein the step of providing a skin conductance signal includes measuring a skin conductance on a palmar side of a wrist of the user. 10. Method according to claim 1, wherein the step of providing a skin conductance signal includes measuring a skin conductance on a plantar side of a foot of the user. 11. Apparatus configured to control a user' s interaction with a non-medical application, the apparatus comprising measuring equipment for measuring skin conductance at an area of the user' s skin, communicatively connected to a processing device, the processing device being configured to perform the following steps: providing a skin conductance signal measured at an area of the user' s skin through a time interval, counting the number of fluctuation peaks in the skin conductance signal through said time interval, and using the number of fluctuation peaks in the skin conductance signal as a control parameter for controlling the user's interaction with the non-medical application. 12. Apparatus configured to control a user' s interaction with a non-medical application, the apparatus comprising measuring equipment for measuring skin conductance at an area of the user' s skin, communicatively connected to a processing device, the processing device being configured to perform a method as set forth in one of the claims 1- 10.
